title: Deutsche Welle Nicos Weg — Reading Passages
subject: Deutsche Welle Nicos Weg
description: Reading passages: Deutsche Welle Nicos Weg

data.sourcePassage: Nico schaut auf sein Handy. Er hat drei neue Nachrichten von seiner Familie. Er tippt schnell eine Antwort: 'Mir geht es gut!'
data.targetPassage: Nico looks at his phone. He has three new messages from his family. He quickly types a reply: 'I am fine!'

questions.question: Who sends Nico messages?
questions.modelAnswer: His family sends him messages.

questions.question: How many new messages does Nico have?
questions.modelAnswer: He has three new messages.

questions.question: What does Nico do after reading the messages?
questions.modelAnswer: He writes a reply.

questions.question: Why does Nico answer quickly?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to let his family know that he is fine.

questions.question: What can you understand about Nico's relationship with his family?
questions.modelAnswer: He seems close to his family.

questions.question: What tense is used in 'Er hat drei neue Nachrichten' and how do you know?
questions.modelAnswer: It is present tense because 'hat' is the present form of 'haben'.

data.sourcePassage: Oh nein, mein Akku ist fast leer! Ich brauche ein Ladekabel. Wo ist die Steckdose? Ich muss meine Mutter anrufen.
data.targetPassage: Oh no, my battery is almost empty! I need a charging cable. Where is the socket? I have to call my mother.

questions.question: What problem does Nico have?
questions.modelAnswer: His battery is almost empty.

questions.question: What does Nico need?
questions.modelAnswer: He needs a charging cable.

questions.question: Who does he need to call?
questions.modelAnswer: He needs to call his mother.

questions.question: Why is the situation urgent?
questions.modelAnswer: It is urgent because his phone may die before he can call his mother.

questions.question: What can you guess Nico is doing next?
questions.modelAnswer: He will probably look for a plug socket and try to charge his phone.

questions.question: What does the modal verb in 'Ich muss meine Mutter anrufen' show?
questions.modelAnswer: It shows necessity or obligation.

data.sourcePassage: Das Rathaus in der Altstadt ist sehr schön. Nico macht ein Foto mit seinem Smartphone. Er schickt das Bild an Selma.
data.targetPassage: The town hall in the old town is very beautiful. Nico takes a photo with his smartphone. He sends the picture to Selma.

questions.question: What does Nico think is very beautiful?
questions.modelAnswer: He thinks the town hall in the old town is very beautiful.

questions.question: What does Nico do with his smartphone?
questions.modelAnswer: He takes a photo.

questions.question: Who does Nico send the picture to?
questions.modelAnswer: He sends it to Selma.

questions.question: Why does Nico send the photo to Selma?
questions.modelAnswer: He probably wants to share something beautiful with her.

questions.question: What does this suggest about Nico's experience in the city?
questions.modelAnswer: He is interested in the city and enjoys what he sees there.

questions.question: Identify the possessive adjective in 'mit seinem Smartphone'.
questions.modelAnswer: The possessive adjective is 'seinem'.

data.sourcePassage: In der Bibliothek ist es leise. Plötzlich klingelt ein Handy laut. Der Mann sagt: 'Entschuldigung!' und schaltet es schnell aus.
data.targetPassage: It is quiet in the library. Suddenly a phone rings loudly. The man says, 'Sorry!' and quickly switches it off.

questions.question: Where is it quiet?
questions.modelAnswer: It is quiet in the library.

questions.question: What suddenly happens?
questions.modelAnswer: A phone rings loudly.

questions.question: What does the man say?
questions.modelAnswer: He says, 'Sorry!' / 'Entschuldigung!'.

questions.question: Why does the man apologise?
questions.modelAnswer: He apologises because the loud phone disturbs the quiet library.

questions.question: What kind of place is the library shown to be?
questions.modelAnswer: It is a place where people are expected to be quiet.

questions.question: What kind of verb is 'ausschalten' in this passage?
questions.modelAnswer: It is a separable verb.

data.sourcePassage: Nico sucht den Weg zum Bahnhof. Er benutzt eine App auf seinem Handy. Die Karte zeigt: 'Gehen Sie zweihundert Meter geradeaus.'
data.targetPassage: Nico is looking for the way to the station. He uses an app on his phone. The map shows: 'Walk straight ahead for two hundred metres.'

questions.question: What is Nico looking for?
questions.modelAnswer: He is looking for the way to the station.

questions.question: What does Nico use on his phone?
questions.modelAnswer: He uses an app.

questions.question: What instruction does the map give?
questions.modelAnswer: It tells him to go straight ahead for two hundred metres.

questions.question: Why is the app useful for Nico?
questions.modelAnswer: It helps him find the correct route to the station.

questions.question: What can you guess about Nico's knowledge of the area?
questions.modelAnswer: He probably does not know the area well.

questions.question: What form is 'Gehen Sie' in the instruction?
questions.modelAnswer: It is the formal imperative / command form.

data.sourcePassage: Hallo, ich heiße Nico. Ich komme aus Spanien. Ich lerne Deutsch in Deutschland. Wie heißt du und woher kommst du?
data.targetPassage: Hello, my name is Nico. I come from Spain. I am learning German in Germany. What is your name and where do you come from?

questions.question: What is Nico's name?
questions.modelAnswer: His name is Nico.

questions.question: Where does Nico come from?
questions.modelAnswer: He comes from Spain.

questions.question: What language is Nico learning?
questions.modelAnswer: He is learning German.

questions.question: Why does Nico ask the other person questions too?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to introduce himself and start a conversation.

questions.question: What does this tell you about Nico?
questions.modelAnswer: He seems friendly and open to meeting people.

questions.question: What person and tense is 'heiße'?
questions.modelAnswer: It is first person singular in the present tense.

data.sourcePassage: Entschuldigung, ich verstehe das Wort nicht. Können Sie das bitte wiederholen? Sprechen Sie auch Englisch oder Spanisch?
data.targetPassage: Excuse me, I do not understand the word. Could you repeat that, please? Do you also speak English or Spanish?

questions.question: What does Nico not understand?
questions.modelAnswer: He does not understand the word.

questions.question: What does Nico ask the person to do?
questions.modelAnswer: He asks the person to repeat it.

questions.question: Which other languages does Nico mention?
questions.modelAnswer: He mentions English and Spanish.

questions.question: Why does Nico ask if the person speaks English or Spanish?
questions.modelAnswer: He hopes communication will be easier in another language.

questions.question: What challenge is Nico facing here?
questions.modelAnswer: He is facing a language barrier.

questions.question: Why is 'Sie' written with a capital letter in this passage?
questions.modelAnswer: Because it is the formal form of 'you' in German.

data.sourcePassage: Nico schreibt eine E-Mail an sein Sprachinstitut. Er schreibt: 'Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, ich möchte mich für den Kurs anmelden.'
data.targetPassage: Nico writes an email to his language institute. He writes: 'Dear Sir or Madam, I would like to register for the course.'

questions.question: What is Nico writing?
questions.modelAnswer: He is writing an email.

questions.question: Who is Nico writing to?
questions.modelAnswer: He is writing to his language institute.

questions.question: What does Nico want to do?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to register for the course.

questions.question: Why does Nico use 'Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren'?
questions.modelAnswer: Because he is writing formally to an organisation.

questions.question: What does this suggest about Nico's plans?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to study German seriously.

questions.question: What does the modal-like expression 'möchte' show in this sentence?
questions.modelAnswer: It expresses a polite wish or intention.

data.sourcePassage: Nico trifft seine Nachbarin im Treppenhaus. Sie sagt: 'Guten Tag! Wie geht es Ihnen?' Nico antwortet: 'Gut, danke! Und Ihnen?'
data.targetPassage: Nico meets his neighbour in the stairwell. She says: 'Good day! How are you?' Nico answers: 'Good, thank you! And you?'

questions.question: Who does Nico meet?
questions.modelAnswer: He meets his female neighbour.

questions.question: Where do they meet?
questions.modelAnswer: They meet in the stairwell.

questions.question: What does Nico answer?
questions.modelAnswer: He says, 'Good, thank you! And you?'

questions.question: What kind of conversation is this?
questions.modelAnswer: It is a polite, everyday greeting.

questions.question: How do you know the exchange is formal?
questions.modelAnswer: Because they use 'Ihnen', the formal form.

questions.question: What is the function of 'Ihnen' in this exchange?
questions.modelAnswer: It is the formal dative form used in the polite question and reply.

data.sourcePassage: Am Abend telefoniert Nico mit seinem Bruder via Skype. Sie sprechen über das Wetter und das Essen in Deutschland. Die Verbindung ist gut.
data.targetPassage: In the evening Nico talks to his brother via Skype. They talk about the weather and the food in Germany. The connection is good.

questions.question: Who does Nico talk to in the evening?
questions.modelAnswer: He talks to his brother.

questions.question: What do they talk about?
questions.modelAnswer: They talk about the weather and the food in Germany.

questions.question: How is the connection?
questions.modelAnswer: The connection is good.

questions.question: Why might Nico call his brother in the evening?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to stay in touch with his family and share his experiences.

questions.question: What does the call suggest about Nico's life in Germany?
questions.modelAnswer: He is adjusting to life there and talks about everyday experiences.

questions.question: What tense is used in 'Sie sprechen über das Wetter'?
questions.modelAnswer: It is in the present tense.

data.sourcePassage: Im Supermarkt gibt es viele moderne Kassen. Man kann mit Karte oder mit dem Handy bezahlen. Das geht sehr schnell und einfach.
data.targetPassage: In the supermarket there are many modern checkouts. You can pay by card or with your phone. It is very quick and easy.

questions.question: Where are there many modern checkouts?
questions.modelAnswer: In the supermarket.

questions.question: How can people pay there?
questions.modelAnswer: They can pay by card or with a phone.

questions.question: How is the payment process described?
questions.modelAnswer: It is very quick and easy.

questions.question: Why might customers like this system?
questions.modelAnswer: Because it saves time and is convenient.

questions.question: What does this tell you about shopping today?
questions.modelAnswer: Shopping is becoming more modern and digital.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'es gibt' mean and how is it used here?
questions.modelAnswer: It means 'there is/there are' and it introduces what is available in the supermarket.

data.sourcePassage: Nico sitzt im Café. Er fragt die Kellnerin: 'Haben Sie hier WLAN?' Sie gibt ihm das Passwort. Jetzt kann er im Internet surfen.
data.targetPassage: Nico is sitting in the café. He asks the waitress: 'Do you have Wi-Fi here?' She gives him the password. Now he can browse the internet.

questions.question: Where is Nico sitting?
questions.modelAnswer: He is sitting in a café.

questions.question: What does Nico ask for?
questions.modelAnswer: He asks about the Wi‑Fi / WLAN.

questions.question: What does the waitress give him?
questions.modelAnswer: She gives him the password.

questions.question: Why does Nico want the Wi‑Fi?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to use the internet.

questions.question: What does this show about cafés today?
questions.modelAnswer: They often provide digital services like Wi‑Fi.

questions.question: What modal verb is used in 'Jetzt kann er im Internet surfen' and what does it show?
questions.modelAnswer: The modal verb is 'kann' and it shows ability or possibility.

data.sourcePassage: In der Stadt gibt es viele E-Scooter. Man braucht eine App, um sie zu mieten. Nico findet das praktisch für kurze Wege.
data.targetPassage: In the city there are many e-scooters. You need an app to rent them. Nico finds that practical for short journeys.

questions.question: What modern vehicles are in the city?
questions.modelAnswer: There are many e-scooters.

questions.question: What do you need to rent them?
questions.modelAnswer: You need an app.

questions.question: What does Nico think about this?
questions.modelAnswer: He thinks it is practical.

questions.question: Why are e-scooters useful for short journeys?
questions.modelAnswer: They are a quick and convenient way to travel short distances.

questions.question: What does this suggest about transport in modern cities?
questions.modelAnswer: Transport is becoming more flexible and app-based.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'um sie zu mieten' in the sentence?
questions.modelAnswer: It is an infinitive clause showing purpose: 'in order to rent them'.

data.sourcePassage: Nico braucht ein neues T-Shirt. Er kauft es nicht im Geschäft, sondern online. Das Paket kommt in zwei Tagen nach Hause.
data.targetPassage: Nico needs a new T-shirt. He does not buy it in the shop, but online. The parcel arrives at home in two days.

questions.question: What does Nico need?
questions.modelAnswer: He needs a new T-shirt.

questions.question: Where does he buy it?
questions.modelAnswer: He buys it online.

questions.question: When does the parcel arrive?
questions.modelAnswer: It arrives in two days.

questions.question: Why might Nico choose to buy online instead of in a shop?
questions.modelAnswer: It may be easier or more convenient for him.

questions.question: What does this tell you about modern shopping habits?
questions.modelAnswer: People often buy things online and wait for delivery.

questions.question: What is the function of 'sondern' in this sentence?
questions.modelAnswer: It contrasts two ideas: not in the shop, but online.

data.sourcePassage: Nico hört gerne Podcasts und Musik. Er benutzt Kopfhörer in der U-Bahn. So ist die Fahrt nicht langweilig.
data.targetPassage: Nico likes listening to podcasts and music. He uses headphones on the underground. That way the journey is not boring.

questions.question: What does Nico like listening to?
questions.modelAnswer: He likes listening to podcasts and music.

questions.question: What does he use on the underground?
questions.modelAnswer: He uses headphones.

questions.question: How is the journey described?
questions.modelAnswer: It is not boring.

questions.question: Why does Nico use headphones on the underground?
questions.modelAnswer: He wants to listen privately and make the journey more enjoyable.

questions.question: What does this show about Nico's travel habits?
questions.modelAnswer: He uses media to make travel time more pleasant.

questions.question: What role does 'gerne' play in the sentence 'Nico hört gerne Podcasts und Musik'?
questions.modelAnswer: It is an adverb showing that he likes doing the action.
